title: lichen_l2_send uses tx_ipv6_buf size check before mutex but copies after mutex
description:
Location: lichen_l2.c:221-237

**What goes wrong:** The size check at line 223 compares pkt_len against sizeof(tx_ipv6_buf), but this happens BEFORE acquiring tx_mutex at line 228. If two threads call lichen_l2_send() with packets that individually fit but together would interleave in the buffer, the first thread's check passes, then the second thread's check passes, then both try to copy into tx_ipv6_buf.

Actually no - each thread acquires the mutex before copying. The size check is just an early-out optimization. Once the mutex is held, only one thread can be in the copy section. The buffer is only used by the thread holding the mutex.

Not a bug - mutex protects the copy operation. Retracting.
close_reason: Not a bug - mutex protects the copy operation and buffer access. Early size check is just optimization to fail fast without blocking.
